response.justification: Over 14 years of progressive full-stack experience with a strong recent track record in TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, and AWS — the core of this role's stack. Active AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) directly satisfies the AI agent requirement, which many candidates lack. The main gaps are the absence of explicit Nest.js and Kotlin experience, and no Python mentioned anywhere on the resume, but the depth of adjacent skills and a clear testing culture make this a compelling interview candidate.
strengths: Deep TypeScript/Node.js/React stack match with production experience at scale (Optilyz, RIDE Capital, re:cinq)
Current hands-on AI agent and MCP tooling work directly satisfies the emerging AI agent development requirement
Strong testing discipline demonstrated through original testing libraries, TDD workshops, and community talks — above average for the market
concerns: No mention of Nest.js anywhere in the resume — the primary backend framework for this role
Kotlin is absent from the resume; Java appears in skills but without prominent recent use in backend services
Python is not listed in skills or projects, despite the role requiring proficiency in it
